cases.title: Bank / Zahlungsdienstleister
cases.profileText:
project name: NovaPay Retail Banking
description: Core banking and payment processing platform for retail customers, offering account management and SEPA/card payments.
signals: financial-sector, personal-data, pii-classified
[application] Payment Initiation Service (application_service, PII): initiates SEPA and card payments on behalf of retail customers.
[application] Customer Account Ledger (data_object, PII): stores account balances, IBANs and transaction history.
[business] Retail Banking Operations (business_process): manages customer onboarding and day-to-day account servicing.
[technology] Core Banking Platform (node): hosts the ledger and payment-processing systems for the bank.
cases.notes: PSD2 (strong customer authentication, open-banking access) is Stage-A-blind — only the corpus can surface it; DORA and GDPR are both rule-covered and corpus-supported.

cases.title: Regional-Klinik mit Patientenportal
cases.profileText:
project name: Lakeside Regional Hospital
description: Regional hospital group operating a patient portal and a connected diagnostic imaging device product line sold to partner clinics.
signals: personal-data, pii-classified, health-data
[application] Patient Portal (application_service, PII): lets patients view lab results and diagnostic imaging reports online.
[technology] Diagnostic Imaging Device Software (system_software): controls a CE-marked medical imaging device deployed at partner clinics.
[business] Patient Care Pathway (business_process): coordinates diagnosis and treatment records across departments.
cases.notes: MDR (medical device software, conformity assessment, post-market surveillance) is Stage-A-blind — GDPR is rule-covered via the health-data/PII signals.

cases.title: IoT-Gerätehersteller
cases.profileText:
project name: GridSense Smart Metering
description: Manufacturer of connected smart-home sensors and gateway devices with cloud telemetry and remote firmware updates.
signals: connected-products, cloud-services, personal-data
[technology] Smart Meter Fleet (device): connected products transmitting usage readings and diagnostics to the cloud.
[application] Telemetry Ingest Service (application_service): receives sensor readings and device health data from the field.
[application] Firmware Update Service (application_component): pushes signed firmware updates to deployed devices, remediating known vulnerabilities.
[business] Customer Registration (business_object, PII): stores device-owner name and address for warranty support.
cases.notes: CRA (essential cybersecurity requirements, vulnerability handling for connected products) is Stage-A-blind; Data Act and GDPR are both rule-covered.

cases.title: AI-Recruiting-SaaS
cases.profileText:
project name: HireLens Screening Platform
description: SaaS platform that automatically screens and ranks job applicants for corporate HR departments across the EU.
signals: ai-components, high-risk-ai-context, personal-data
[application] CV Scoring Model (ai_agent): ranks candidate resumes using a trained scoring model before human review.
[business] Recruiting Process (business_process): hiring workflow from application intake to offer decision.
[application] Applicant Data Store (data_object, PII): stores resumes, contact data and interview notes for each candidate.
cases.notes: Both families are rule-covered — this case is a Stage-A/Stage-B agreement check, not a rule-less discovery test.

cases.title: Reines internes CRM (Precision-Fall)
cases.profileText:
project name: Internal Sales CRM
description: Internal CRM used by the domestic sales team to track customer contacts and the deal pipeline, no AI, single-country deployment.
signals: personal-data, pii-classified, customer-facing
[application] Customer Relationship Management (application_component, PII): stores customer contact details, deal stage and communication history.
[business] Sales Process (business_process): manages the deal pipeline from lead to close for domestic accounts only.
cases.notes: Precision case — deliberately narrow architecture. A good retriever should NOT surface dora/nis2/ai-act/mdr here despite generic business-software language; only GDPR (personal data in a CRM) applies.

cases.title: Energienetz-Betreiber
cases.profileText:
project name: RegioGrid Distribution
description: Regional electricity grid operator running SCADA systems for substation monitoring and control, serving critical infrastructure customers.
signals: critical-sector, security-baseline, personal-data
[technology] SCADA Control System (node): monitors and controls substations across the regional distribution grid.
[business] Grid Operations (business_process): balances load and manages outages for critical infrastructure.
[application] Customer Contract Management (application_component, PII): stores household customer contracts and metering data.
cases.notes: Both families are rule-covered — NIS2 via the critical-sector/security-baseline signals, GDPR via household customer data.

cases.title: Automotive-Telematik
cases.profileText:
project name: FleetLink Telematics Unit
description: Automotive OEM telematics unit collecting vehicle diagnostics and location data, connected via cellular network to a fleet backend.
signals: connected-products, personal-data, cloud-services
[technology] Telematics Control Unit (device): in-vehicle unit transmitting diagnostic and location data over a cellular connection.
[application] Fleet Backend Service (application_service): aggregates vehicle data for fleet operators and dealers.
[application] OTA Update Manager (application_component): manages over-the-air software updates and monitors the vehicle's cybersecurity posture for type approval.
cases.notes: UNECE R155 (vehicle type-approval cybersecurity management system) is Stage-A-blind; GDPR and Data Act are both rule-covered.

cases.title: E-Commerce mit Tracking
cases.profileText:
project name: UrbanCart Online Shop
description: Online shop using tracking cookies and newsletter marketing, storing customer accounts and order history for EU consumers.
signals: personal-data, customer-facing
[application] Web Storefront (application_component, PII): customer accounts and browsing behaviour tracked via cookies for retargeting.
[application] Marketing Automation (application_service): sends newsletter emails and behavioural ad campaigns using tracking pixels.
[business] Order Fulfillment (business_process): processes customer orders and payment for the online shop.
cases.notes: ePrivacy (cookie consent, unsolicited marketing communications) is Stage-A-blind; GDPR is rule-covered via the personal-data signal.

cases.title: Cloud-MSP mit Finanzkunden
cases.profileText:
project name: NimbusOps Managed Cloud
description: Managed service provider hosting workloads for enterprise clients, including several regulated financial-sector customers, across the EU.
signals: critical-sector, cloud-services, personal-data
[technology] Multi-Tenant Cloud Platform (node): hosts customer workloads including systems belonging to banking clients.
[business] Client Onboarding (business_process): manages contracts with enterprise customers across multiple sectors.
[application] Access Management Service (application_component, PII): manages administrator credentials and client contact data.
cases.notes: DORA is plausible only indirectly (as an ICT third-party provider to financial clients, not itself a financial entity) — deliberately excluded from goldFamilies pending Owner review; a retriever surfacing dora here is not necessarily wrong.

cases.title: Offline-Handwerksbetrieb ohne IT (Hard Negative)
cases.profileText:
project name: Holzwerk Mueller
description: Small carpentry workshop with no digital systems, paper-based scheduling and invoicing, serving local private customers only.
[business] Workshop Scheduling (business_process): paper appointment book used to plan custom furniture orders.
[business] Manual Invoicing (business_object): handwritten invoices filed in physical binders at the end of each month.
cases.notes: Hard negative — no IT estate, no meaningful personal-data processing beyond a paper appointment book. A retriever should not surface any law family with meaningful confidence here.

cases.title: Medizin-KI-Diagnostik
cases.profileText:
project name: RadAssist Diagnostic AI
description: Startup building an AI-based diagnostic support system that analyzes radiology images for partner hospitals across the EU.
signals: ai-components, high-risk-ai-context, personal-data, health-data
[application] Diagnostic AI Model (ai_agent): analyzes CT and MRI scans to flag suspected anomalies for radiologists.
[application] Imaging Data Store (data_object, PII): stores patient scans and diagnostic reports for review.
[business] Radiology Review Workflow (business_process): clinician review and sign-off of AI-flagged findings before diagnosis.
cases.notes: MDR (software as a medical device, conformity assessment for diagnostic AI) is Stage-A-blind; AI Act and GDPR are both rule-covered — a two-family Stage-A/B agreement plus one rule-less family.

cases.title: Lieferketten-Konzern
cases.profileText:
project name: Meridian Industrial Group
description: Large industrial group with over 3000 employees and a multi-tier global supply chain sourcing raw materials for manufacturing.
signals: supply-chain, personal-data
[business] Supplier Risk Assessment (business_process): evaluates human-rights and environmental risk across tier-1 and tier-2 suppliers.
[application] Supplier Master Data (data_object, PII): stores supplier contact persons and compliance certificates.
[business] Procurement (business_process): manages purchase orders across the global supply chain.
cases.notes: Both families are rule-covered — LKSG via the supply-chain signal (employee-count threshold assumed met), GDPR via supplier contact data.
